OxyMetaG_Graphs

Joey Lin

2026-06-05

Today, I will try to make some graphs for the oxymetag data i have on the bumblebees. My first graph will be analyzing the oxygen level of bumblebee in Laguna Beach from March to August. I hypothesize that the level of oxygen will increase as the lifecycle of the bumblebee.

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
merged <- read_csv("Data/merged_oxymetag.csv")
## Rows: 113 Columns: 23
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (15): sample_id, date, subgenus, species_id, id_certainty, morphotype, c...
## dbl  (8): ratio, aerobe_pfams, anaerobe_pfams, Oxygen_level, year, lat, lon,...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
laguna <- merged %>%
  filter(year == 2022) %>%
  mutate(date_full = as.Date(paste0("2022", date), format = "%Y%m%d"))

ggplot(laguna, aes(x = date_full, y = Oxygen_level)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE) +
  labs(
    title = "Gut Microbiome Oxygen Level Over Time",
    subtitle = "Bombus samples from Laguna Beach Botanical Garden (2022)",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Predicted Oxygen Level (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Running Regression to check significance.

# Convert date to numeric for regression
laguna <- laguna %>%
  mutate(date_numeric = as.numeric(date_full))

# Run linear regression
model <- lm(Oxygen_level ~ date_numeric, data = laguna)
summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = Oxygen_level ~ date_numeric, data = laguna)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -43.754  -8.864   8.237   8.765  14.007 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)  -633.26297 1515.03660  -0.418    0.682
## date_numeric    0.03772    0.07902   0.477    0.640
## 
## Residual standard error: 15.07 on 14 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.01601,    Adjusted R-squared:  -0.05427 
## F-statistic: 0.2278 on 1 and 14 DF,  p-value: 0.6405
# Add log transformed date
laguna <- laguna %>%
  mutate(date_log = log(date_numeric))

# Run log regression
model_log <- lm(Oxygen_level ~ date_log, data = laguna)
summary(model_log)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = Oxygen_level ~ date_log, data = laguna)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -43.750  -8.870   8.244   8.769  14.000 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)  -7014.2    14925.7  -0.470    0.646
## date_log       720.4     1513.6   0.476    0.641
## 
## Residual standard error: 15.07 on 14 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.01592,    Adjusted R-squared:  -0.05437 
## F-statistic: 0.2265 on 1 and 14 DF,  p-value: 0.6414

Results: not signifciant

Elevation and Oxygen Level

Now, I will do regression of elevation oxygen and elevation aerobe pfams

ggplot(merged, aes(x = elev_masl, y = Oxygen_level)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Elevation vs Oxygen Level",
    x = "Elevation (masl)",
    y = "Oxygen Level"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 35 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Warning: Removed 35 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

# Linear regression
model_linear <- lm(Oxygen_level ~ elev_masl, data = merged)
summary(model_linear)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = Oxygen_level ~ elev_masl, data = merged)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -30.5619  -8.0914  -0.6941   6.1617  26.6127 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 88.860124   4.713722  18.851   <2e-16 ***
## elev_masl   -0.004540   0.002017  -2.251   0.0272 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 14.02 on 76 degrees of freedom
##   (35 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.06253,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.05019 
## F-statistic: 5.069 on 1 and 76 DF,  p-value: 0.02725
# Log regression
model_log <- lm(aerobe_pfams ~ log(Oxygen_level), data = merged)
summary(model_log)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = aerobe_pfams ~ log(Oxygen_level), data = merged)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.9599 -1.3285  0.1011  1.1519  3.2474 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)        14.9196     3.8560   3.869 0.000204 ***
## log(Oxygen_level)  -1.7284     0.8809  -1.962 0.052778 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 1.693 on 92 degrees of freedom
##   (19 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.04016,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.02973 
## F-statistic:  3.85 on 1 and 92 DF,  p-value: 0.05278

Result: elevation does not seem to explain the variation of oxygen levels in bumblebees (low p value but low r^2 value).

#Try multiple regression to increase r^2

model_multi <- lm(Oxygen_level ~ elev_masl + species_id + country, data = merged)
summary(model_multi)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = Oxygen_level ~ elev_masl + species_id + country, 
##     data = merged)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -26.6663  -4.5665  -0.5995   4.3922  26.6663 
## 
## Coefficients: (1 not defined because of singularities)
##                                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                      5.470e+01  9.107e+00   6.006 1.20e-07 ***
## elev_masl                       -2.944e-05  2.980e-03  -0.010 0.992151    
## species_idfervidus               3.018e+01  6.491e+00   4.649 1.88e-05 ***
## species_idflavifrons_dimidiatus  4.536e+01  1.204e+01   3.769 0.000377 ***
## species_idflavifrons_flavifrons  1.871e+01  9.263e+00   2.020 0.047894 *  
## species_idfunebris               1.930e+01  8.064e+00   2.393 0.019875 *  
## species_idgriseocollis           4.536e+01  1.204e+01   3.766 0.000380 ***
## species_idhortulanus             2.607e+01  7.597e+00   3.431 0.001095 ** 
## species_idhuntii                 3.089e+01  6.760e+00   4.569 2.49e-05 ***
## species_idinsularis              4.538e+01  1.201e+01   3.780 0.000364 ***
## species_idmorrisoni              2.829e+01  6.329e+00   4.469 3.54e-05 ***
## species_idnevadensis             1.586e+01  9.464e+00   1.676 0.099038 .  
## species_idpensylvanicus          2.648e+01  8.589e+00   3.083 0.003095 ** 
## species_idrobustus               3.992e+00  7.168e+00   0.557 0.579655    
## species_idrubicundus             2.677e+01  7.823e+00   3.422 0.001125 ** 
## species_idrufocinctus            4.537e+01  1.196e+01   3.794 0.000347 ***
## species_idsonorus                1.816e+01  6.725e+00   2.700 0.009008 ** 
## species_idvandykei               4.535e+01  9.591e+00   4.728 1.42e-05 ***
## countryUS                               NA         NA      NA       NA    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 10.69 on 60 degrees of freedom
##   (35 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.5702, Adjusted R-squared:  0.4485 
## F-statistic: 4.683 on 17 and 60 DF,  p-value: 4.057e-06

Plot species vs Oxygen level

ggplot(merged, aes(x = reorder(species_id, Oxygen_level, median, na.rm = TRUE), 
                   y = Oxygen_level, fill = country)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Gut Microbiome Oxygen Level by Species",
    x = "Species",
    y = "Predicted Oxygen Level (%)",
    fill = "Country"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
## Warning: Removed 19 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_boxplot()`).

Results: Species type seems to explain more variation (adjusted r2 = 0.4485). When we isolate elevation from other factors, it does not seem statistically signifant (r^2 = 0.99).
